---
title: "Teaching"
title-block-style: none
pagetitle: "Teaching | Mesfin Genie"
description: "Teaching portfolio and behavioural economics resources."
page-layout: full
image: /assets/img/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell"><p class="eyebrow">Teaching</p><h1>Health economics and behavioural economics teaching</h1><p class="lead">An evidence-based teaching portfolio in health economics, finance and behavioural economics at the University of Newcastle.</p></div></header><section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><h2>Courses</h2><div class="grid-3"><article class="course-card"><div class="course-code">ECON3011</div><h3>Introduction to Health Economics and Finance</h3><p><strong>Primary Course Coordinator</strong>, The University of Newcastle</p><p>22 July 2024–29 November 2024</p><p>Foundations of health economics, health financing, demand for healthcare, insurance and introductory economic evaluation.</p></article><article class="course-card"><div class="course-code">ECON2112</div><h3>Health Economics and Finance</h3><p><strong>Lecturer and Course Coordinator / Primary Course Coordinator</strong>, The University of Newcastle</p><p>2025–2026 coordination; 2025 teaching record</p><p>Healthcare markets, provider and patient behaviour, public and private finance, economic evaluation and health-policy decisions.</p></article><article class="course-card"><div class="course-code">ECON3111</div><h3>Behavioural Economics</h3><p><strong>Lecturer and Course Coordinator / Primary Course Coordinator</strong>, The University of Newcastle</p><p>2025–2026 coordination; 2025 teaching record</p><p>Rational-choice benchmarks, behavioural mechanisms, decision-making under risk and uncertainty, experiments and behavioural public policy.</p></article></div></div></section><section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Open teaching resources</p><h2>Behavioural economics</h2></div><a class="button-text" href="/teaching/behavioural-economics/">View resources →</a></div><div class="grid-2"><article class="resource-card"><img src="/assets/teaching/week1-foundations.webp" width="900" height="506" alt="Thumbnail of Week 1 behavioural economics teaching slide"><h3>Foundations and rational choice under certainty</h3><p>Benchmark models, preferences, utility, rational choice and the difference between normative and descriptive accounts of decision-making.</p></article><article class="resource-card"><img src="/assets/teaching/week6-risk-uncertainty.webp" width="900" height="506" alt="Thumbnail of Week 6 behavioural economics teaching slide"><h3>Advanced choice under risk and uncertainty</h3><p>Expected utility, Allais and Ellsberg paradoxes, prospect theory, probability weighting, loss aversion and ambiguity.</p></article></div></div></section>
```
